A petition signed by every prop,
erty owners but / effected by the
proposed new spur tracks of the
Northern Pacific railroad on East
Riverside av. will be presented to
the council, protesting against the
spurs.


Tuesday night Councilman
Brown, DR.. E. e. Pittwood and At
torney Edward j. Cannon, of the
Northern Pacific, assured the coun,
ell that those people who had pre-.
viously objected now desired the
spurs. They seem to have made a
very marked and peculiar miscalcu-
lation.


Cannon, being the railroad attor-
ney, may have been easily decOyed
into erroneous views, and it ap-
pears that Councilman Brown as
well as DR.. Pittwood were likewise
imposed upon. Shame on the man
that deluded them so.
